source_scope: The 25 academic references plus two contextual sources R0-R26 cited by public ChatGPT share 6a58edb7-547c-83ea-ae8a-b6f62d3b283a; academic metadata verified against canonical DOI and arXiv records.

sources.title: Can Tiny Models Actually Reason?
sources.summary: Motivating discussion that proposes combining explicit lattice state, recursive refinement, selective stochastic search, and diverse trajectories.

metadata.feedback_claim: Decompose the proposed stack into independently falsifiable hard-state, recursion, search, and diversity levers.
metadata.limitations: A video discussion is not academic evidence and its claims do not transfer to OpenUI.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: A decomposed matrix can attribute any gain more reliably than a single combined-stack run.

sources.title: Abstract Interpretation: A Unified Lattice Model for Static Analysis of Programs by Construction or Approximation of Fixpoints
sources.summary: Introduces a lattice-theoretic framework for sound program analysis and fixpoint approximation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Keep hard partial information and ordering explicit.
metadata.repo_relevance: CompletionForest as finite hard candidate state
metadata.limitations: The OpenUI controller does not establish a Galois connection or general soundness theorem.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Materializing the compiler forest before neural ranking preserves legal alternatives that score-first decoding can hide.

sources.title: The Reduced Product of Abstract Domains and the Combination of Decision Procedures
sources.summary: Relates reduced products of abstract domains to combinations of decision procedures.

metadata.feedback_claim: Combine hard domains by reduction without confusing heuristic preference with deduction.
metadata.repo_relevance: Grammar, schema, binder, symbol, and slot constraints
metadata.limitations: No formal reduced product of OpenUI domains is constructed.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Conflict-local reduction across existing compiler domains can prune impossible branches without false elimination.

sources.title: Hierarchical Reasoning Model
sources.summary: Uses interdependent recurrent modules operating at different timescales for iterative reasoning.

metadata.feedback_claim: Repeated computation can substitute for parameter scale on structured tasks.
metadata.repo_relevance: Bounded recursive calls to the existing denoiser
metadata.limitations: No HRM hierarchy, objective, or benchmark recipe is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Extra recurrent proposals help only when the compiler state reports ambiguity or failure.

sources.title: Are Your Reasoning Models Reasoning or Guessing? A Mechanistic Analysis of Hierarchical Reasoning Models
sources.summary: Finds multiple fixed points and abrupt solution dynamics in HRM, motivating behavioral ablations and multiple guesses.

metadata.feedback_claim: A recurrent model can converge to a wrong fixed point; mechanism labels require behavioral tests.
metadata.repo_relevance: Stagnation detection and matched trajectory controls
metadata.limitations: Findings on HRM puzzle tasks do not establish OpenUI decoder failure modes.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: A reachable no-progress signature predicts cases where a bounded alternative trajectory is useful.

sources.title: Less is More: Recursive Reasoning with Tiny Networks
sources.summary: Refines a latent state and answer through repeated use of one small shared network.

metadata.feedback_claim: Reuse a small transition function for iterative refinement.
metadata.limitations: No TRM training recipe or Q head is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Compiler-conditioned recurrence improves ambiguous choices without changing singleton decisions.

sources.title: Probabilistic Tiny Recursive Model
sources.summary: Adds stochastic recursive trajectories and selects among them to escape suboptimal deterministic solutions.

metadata.feedback_claim: Selective stochasticity can escape bad recursive basins at test time.
metadata.limitations: The repository has no PTRM latent dynamics or trained Q selector.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Bottom/stagnation-triggered width four matches always-on valid yield with fewer calls.

sources.title: Lattice Deduction Transformers
sources.summary: Alternates recurrent neural updates with lattice projection and search over abstract solution candidates.

metadata.feedback_claim: Neural proposals should be projected through explicit hard candidate state and may abstain.
metadata.limitations: No LDT architecture, alpha supervision, on-policy search-state training, or reported accuracy is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Bounded rollback over an explicit legal forest reduces invalid terminal outputs without false hard elimination.

sources.title: Lattice Deduction Transformers reproduction repository
sources.summary: A curated reconstruction of LDT with disclosed evaluation corrections and ongoing end-to-end validation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Implementation and evaluation details require independent verification.
metadata.repo_relevance: Comparison target and caution for claimed benchmark parity
metadata.limitations: This is a community reconstruction, not a stable author implementation; it reports corrected Sudoku and maze evaluation details.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: OpenUI results should report abstention and false-elimination evidence instead of inheriting LDT headline accuracy.

sources.title: Generative Recursive Reasoning
sources.summary: Models recursive reasoning as stochastic latent trajectories supporting multiple hypotheses and inference-time scaling.

metadata.feedback_claim: Parallel recursive trajectories can represent multimodal solution processes.
metadata.repo_relevance: Validated OpenUI trajectory sampling and AST deduplication
metadata.limitations: No GRAM variational training or latent generative model is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: AST-deduplicated terminal trajectories yield more semantically distinct valid programs than raw string deduplication.

sources.title: Structured Denoising Diffusion Models in Discrete State-Spaces
sources.summary: Generalizes discrete diffusion with structured transition matrices and absorbing states.

metadata.feedback_claim: Discrete refinement behavior depends on the corruption and transition structure.
metadata.limitations: Compiler search is not a D3PM objective.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Compiler-local remasking is a better search intervention than uniform whole-sequence perturbation.

sources.title: Simple and Effective Masked Diffusion Language Models
sources.summary: Presents a simple absorbing-mask diffusion language-model objective and recipe.

metadata.feedback_claim: A simple shared denoiser can support iterative masked refinement.
metadata.limitations: It does not define compiler lattices, rollback, or OpenUI semantics.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Search improvements can be evaluated inference-only without changing the base denoising objective.

sources.title: DiffuCoder: Understanding and Improving Masked Diffusion Models for Code Generation
sources.summary: Studies order-flexible masked diffusion decoding and reinforcement learning for code generation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Global iterative code refinement exposes diverse generation orders.
metadata.limitations: No 7B DiffuCoder model, coupled-GRPO, or benchmark result transfers.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Compiler-triggered alternative ordering improves code structure at lower cost than always-on diversification.

sources.title: TreeDiff: AST-Guided Code Generation with Diffusion LLMs
sources.summary: Uses AST-aware corruption to expose syntactic boundaries and hierarchical dependencies during code diffusion.

metadata.feedback_claim: Structure-aware interventions are preferable to random token masking for code.
metadata.repo_relevance: Parser-local conflict cones and AST fingerprints
metadata.limitations: No TreeDiff model or training objective is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Rollback localized to the implicated decision preserves more valid structure than broad suffix replacement.

sources.title: DINGO: Constrained Inference for Diffusion LLMs
sources.summary: Applies dynamic programming to enforce regular-language constraints during parallel diffusion inference.

metadata.feedback_claim: Constraints should intervene during denoising rather than only after generation.
metadata.limitations: No DINGO dynamic program or distribution-preservation proof is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Interleaved compiler projection reduces terminal invalidity compared with final-only validation.

sources.title: Constrained Decoding of Diffusion LLMs with Context-Free Grammars
sources.summary: Checks CFG completion feasibility for multiple masked regions during diffusion decoding.

metadata.feedback_claim: Reject a partial fill only when its completion language is empty.
metadata.repo_relevance: Existing incremental OpenUI acceptor and completion forest
metadata.limitations: The repository uses an OpenUI specialization, not full CFG-intersection emptiness.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Forest emptiness is a safer rollback trigger than low model confidence.

sources.title: Constrained Code Generation with Discrete Diffusion
sources.summary: Uses program analysis and optimization to localize and steer constraint-relevant regions of global diffusion states.

metadata.feedback_claim: Global diffusion states permit localized constraint-aware intervention.
metadata.limitations: No CDC optimizer, functionality, or security result is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Conflict-local nogoods prevent repeated failures with fewer unrelated candidate eliminations.

sources.title: Beyond Execution: Static-Analysis Rewards and Hint-Conditioned Diffusion RL for Code Generation
sources.summary: Uses static-analysis feedback as rewards and hints for diffusion reinforcement learning in code generation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Static-analysis traces can provide denser signals than terminal execution alone.
metadata.repo_relevance: Future search telemetry and reward design
metadata.limitations: This campaign is inference-only and does not run RL.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Rollback and false-elimination telemetry can identify future reward targets without authorizing RL.

sources.title: FLAME: A Small Language Model for Spreadsheet Formulas
sources.summary: Shows the value of a domain-specific small language model for spreadsheet formula generation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Narrow domain structure can make small models competitive.
metadata.limitations: FLAME provides no lattice or recursive search mechanism.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: A specialized compiler can make test-time structured search useful at small model scale.

sources.title: Neurosymbolic Repair for Low-Code Formula Languages
sources.summary: Uses neural localization and ranking with symbolic candidate repair for low-code formulas.

metadata.feedback_claim: Symbolic rules should generate admissible repairs while neural scores rank them.
metadata.limitations: LaMirage and its target languages are not reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Neural ordering can improve selection without ever overriding compiler admissibility.

sources.title: Synchromesh: Reliable code generation from pre-trained language models
sources.summary: Combines completion engines with constrained semantic decoding for reliable code generation.

metadata.feedback_claim: A completion engine can expose contextual semantic admissibility during decoding.
metadata.limitations: Target Similarity Tuning and Synchromesh CSD are not reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Preserving the full completion forest enables safe rollback to alternative semantic branches.

sources.title: Type-Constrained Code Generation with Language Models
sources.summary: Combines prefix automata with type inhabitation search to enforce well-typed generation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Semantic type constraints can participate in prefix decoding.
metadata.repo_relevance: OpenUI schema, binder, symbol, and slot checks
metadata.limitations: OpenUI checks are narrower than the paper's formal and TypeScript systems.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: A conflict scoped to the failing semantic domain avoids pruning grammar-valid siblings.

sources.title: Statically Contextualizing Large Language Models with Typed Holes
sources.summary: Supplies static type and scope context at program holes for code generation.

metadata.feedback_claim: Localized holes should carry the static context needed to repair them.
metadata.repo_relevance: Stable-prefix parser rebuild and failure cones
metadata.limitations: No ChatLSP protocol or general-purpose type service is integrated.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Restoring parser and decision state from the last stable prefix improves repair determinism.

sources.title: Neural-Guided Deductive Search for Real-Time Program Synthesis from Examples
sources.summary: Uses neural predictions to prioritize branches in a deductive synthesis procedure.

metadata.feedback_claim: Learning should prioritize deductive search, not define correctness.
metadata.limitations: The synthesis domain, supervision, and speedups do not transfer.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Score ordering lowers calls to a valid terminal candidate without affecting the legal set.

sources.title: DreamCoder: Growing generalizable, interpretable knowledge with wake-sleep Bayesian program learning
sources.summary: Alternates program search, abstraction learning, and neural search guidance in a wake-sleep loop.

metadata.feedback_claim: Search traces can later improve reusable representations and guidance.
metadata.limitations: No library learning, wake-sleep training, or DreamCoder domain is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Persisted V9 conflict trajectories can define a later training dataset only after inference gains are demonstrated.

sources.title: Flow Network based Generative Models for Non-Iterative Diverse Candidate Generation
sources.summary: Learns policies that sample diverse terminal objects in proportion to reward across multiple construction trajectories.

metadata.feedback_claim: Different trajectories can reach the same terminal object, so semantic diversity should be measured at terminals.
metadata.limitations: GRAM-style inference is not a GFlowNet and has no flow-matching objective.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Unique valid AST count is more informative than raw trajectory count.

sources.title: SATNet: Bridging deep learning and logical reasoning using a differentiable satisfiability solver
sources.summary: Embeds a differentiable approximate MAXSAT solver inside neural systems.

metadata.feedback_claim: Logical structure and neural learning can be combined with a clear solver boundary.
metadata.repo_relevance: Compiler authority versus soft proposal scores
metadata.limitations: No differentiable SAT layer or learned logic is introduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Treating compiler evidence as hard and model scores as soft yields zero false hard eliminations by construction.
